idea: cuBLAS reference baseline
1p2c warp-spec baseline: TM=128 WARP_N=128 stages=3 — starting point
Reduce TILE_M 128->64, switch 1p2c->1p1c: 512 CTAs vs 256, halves SMEM from 163KB to 104KB, 4.5 waves vs 2.25
Persistent 114 CTA STAGES=4 WARP_N=128: SMEM=128KB fills carveout, only 1 CTA/SM, drops occupancy 24%->7.8%, regression -27T
Persistent WARP_N=96 STAGES=3: 2048 not divisible by 96 (22 tiles, last partial), severe boundary overhead, 148T <<< 228T. N=128 required for 2048.
Persistent STAGES=2 WARP_N=128: SMEM=80KB, but 2-stage pipeline insufficient to hide K=16384 memory latency; compute utilization extremely low 139T. Persistent approach abandoned.
1p2c ping-pong TILE_N=256: 2 consumers split N-dim, 256 CTAs. SMEM=188KB too large, reduced occupancy. Reverted.
Phase 2 cu fine-tune: unroll 4 + launch_bounds(256,1). Regression -48T. launch_bounds(256,1) may force register spilling.
TILE_K=128 STAGES=3: CUDA failure invalid argument at runtime. TMA box shape 128 may exceed limits or swizzle mismatch.
WARP_N=256: 211T vs 228T. CTA count dropped to 256 (2.2 waves), wave quantization hurts more than larger N helps.
STAGES=2: 199T vs 228T. 2 stages insufficient to hide K=16384 memory latency, same conclusion as iter005.
STAGES=4: 174T vs 228T. SMEM increased to 131KB, occupancy dropped, pipeline overhead outweighs benefits.
1p2c warp-spec TILE_M=128 STAGES=2: 180T vs 228T. 2 stages insufficient for K=16384, 256 CTAs (2.2 waves) vs 512 (4.5 waves), 1p2c adds sync overhead.
TILE_K=128 STAGES=2: CUDA invalid argument error. TMA cannot handle 128-wide K box, or swizzle mismatch. Same failure as iter008.
SWIZ=32 TILE_K=16: 98T vs 228T. K-loop iterations 4x larger (1024 vs 256), TMA transfers too small, loop overhead dominates. TILE_K=64 is the sweet spot.
maxrregcount=128 via EXTRA_TARGET_CFLAGS: 233T vs 228T (+2.2%). Register limiting improved occupancy while maintaining enough regs for WGMMA. First KEEP since iter002.
maxrregcount=96: 232.7T vs 232.7T (iter015). No improvement from tighter register limit - already at optimal.
WARP_N=64 + maxrregcount=128: 168T vs 233T. Smaller tile per CTA hurts - less work amortizes launch overhead poorly. WARP_N=128 is optimal.
launch_bounds failed to apply (cp error), ran with maxrregcount=128 only: 233.0T vs 232.7T. Confirms iter015 result, no improvement from launch_bounds itself.
WARP_N=192 + maxrregcount=128: 184T vs 233T. Larger SMEM (147KB) reduces occupancy and increases launch overhead. WARP_N=128 remains optimal.
STAGES=2 + maxrregcount=128: 207T vs 233T. Better than iter010 (199T) but still insufficient pipeline depth for K=16384. STAGES=3 remains optimal.
TILE_M=128 1p1c: WGMMA only supports m64. Would need 2 mma accumulators with separate load/store, major code rewrite.
use_fast_math + maxrregcount=128: 232.5T vs 232.7T. No improvement - fast_math has no effect on tensor core ops.
maxrregcount=160: 179T vs 233T. 160 regs forces spilling and reduces occupancy. 128 remains optimal.
